semantic.role: Architectural governance rules that define and enforce the layered architecture constraints for the ArchSpine repository.
responsibilities: Defining the separation between CLI, services, core/tasks/engines, and infra layers.
Specifying error and warning severity rules to prevent layering violations.
Maintaining the documented boundaries for module imports and responsibilities.
outOfScope: Detailed implementation of specific modules or services.
Non-layering code style or naming conventions.

English.purpose: This document establishes the layered architecture constraints for the ArchSpine project, ensuring clear boundaries between entrypoints, orchestration, pipeline, engine, and infrastructure modules.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for all developers and maintainers of the ArchSpine repository, especially those adding new features or refactoring existing code. It serves as a reference for code review and architectural compliance.
key_takeaways: CLI modules must be thin entrypoints without pipeline or persistence logic.
Services orchestrate core/tasks/engines/infra, with view-specific services in src/services/view/.
Core modules define pipeline contracts without depending on CLI.
Task modules implement stage-local work without command parsing.
Engine modules provide reusable analysis without CLI or service dependencies.
Infra modules expose stable facades; prefer facades over deep internal imports.
